Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff For Perjury Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Apr-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF FOR PERJURY

Appearances via Zoom:
David Killoran of Dykem Gossett LLP (669.231.8810, DKilloran@dykema.com) for defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.;
Jerome Bellotti of Harris Personal Injury Lawyers, Inc. (408.512.3600, jerome@harrispersonalinjury.com) for plaintiff Kun Wang;
Paul Buckley of Taylor Anderson LLP (650.561.3061, pbuckley@talawfirm.com) for defendant Hsiang Ling Cheng Yu.

Argued and the court rules as follows: motion for terminating sanctions is denied, although lesser sanctions crafted by the court are ordered. Plaintiff Kun Wang's request for sanctions is denied. The court rejects the unsupported assertions of the plaintiff that his conduct relating to his attendance at and graduation from UC Berkeley and his grades received from San Jose City College are explainable by his alleged traumatic brain injury. Plaintiff has had sufficient opportunity to submit expert testimony supporting his assertions and his failure to do so shows that he intentionally misrepresented that he attended and graduated from UC Berkeley and received far better grades than he actually received from San Jose City College to gain an unwarranted litigation advantage. His doing so is wholly inconsistent with the truth-seeking functions of court adjudication and is strongly condemnable. But it does not, without more, warrant terminating sanctions. The court invited counsel for defendants to draft an instruction to the trier of fact regarding these matters in the context of language from CACI 107 that, based on plaintiff's acknowledged falsehoods, the trier of fact may choose not to believe some or all of plaintiff's testimony on other matters. On review of the proposed instruction, the court approves defendant's proposed jury instruction. The court also requires on a mutually convenient date no later than May 15, 2026 that plaintiff submit to an additional session of his deposition, not to exceed three hours, regarding these matters, including whether he forged or doctored any other documents and/or made any other false statements at his deposition. The costs of the additional session of plaintiff's deposition -- including the court reporter fees and time spent by defense counsel during the deposition at their normal hourly rates -- are to be borne by the plaintiff and to be paid within ten days of the additional session of the deposition. The lesser sanctions crafted by the court are tailored to remediate the discovery abuse committed by the plaintiff. Proposed order submitted. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Lindsey Hart, CSR 14746 (925.389.1860, Lindsey.hartCSR@gmail.com) (301/HEK)
